In the figure, the graph of \(y=2x^2-4x+c\) passes through the point \((1,k)\). Find the value of \(k\).

A. \(-5\)
B. \(-4\)
C. \(-3\)
D. \(-2\)

If the equation \(4x^2+kx+9=0\) has equal positive roots, then \(k=\)
A. \(-6\).
B. \(6\).
C. \(-12\).
D. \(12\).

The solution of \(-2x\lt3-x\) or \(3x+3\gt0\) is
A. \(x\gt-3\).
B. \(x\gt-1\).
C. \(-3\lt x\lt-1\).
D. \(x\lt-3\) or \(x\gt-1\).

Let \(a_n\) be the \(n\)th term of an arithmetic sequence. If \(a_1=10\) and \(a_2=13\), then \(a_{21}+a_{22}+\cdots+a_{30}=\)
A. \(765\).
B. \(835\).
C. \(865\).
D. \(1605\).

The marked price of a book is \(20\%\) above the cost. If the book is sold at a \(10\%\) discount on the marked price, then the percentage profit is
A. \(2\%\).
B. \(8\%\).
C. \(10\%\).
D. \(18\%\).

A box contains two kinds of coins: \(\$5\) and \(\$2\). The ratio of the number of \(\$5\) coins to the number of \(\$2\) coins is \(4:5\). If the total value of the coins is \(\$90\), then the total number of coins in the box is
A. \(9\).
B. \(18\).
C. \(27\).
D. \(36\).

The scale of a map is \(1:20000\). If two buildings are \(3.8\text{ cm}\) apart on the map, then the actual distance between the two buildings is
A. \(0.076\text{ km}\).
B. \(0.76\text{ km}\).
C. \(7.6\text{ km}\).
D. \(76\text{ km}\).

It is known that \(y\) varies partly as \(x\) and partly as \(\sqrt{x}\). When \(x=1\), \(y=4\) and when \(x=4\), \(y=10\). Find \(y\) when \(x=16\).
A. \(28\)
B. \(52\)
C. \(80\)
D. \(256\)

In the figure, \(ABCD\) is a parallelogram and \(E\) is a point on \(AD\) such that \(AE:ED=1:3\). If the area of \(\Delta ABE\) is \(3\text{ cm}^2\), then the area of the shaded region is

A. \(9\text{ cm}^2\).
B. \(15\text{ cm}^2\).
C. \(21\text{ cm}^2\).
D. \(24\text{ cm}^2\).

In the figure, \(AD\) and \(BC\) meet at \(E\). If \(CE:EB=3:1\), then
\(\text{area of }\Delta ABD:\text{area of }\Delta CDE=\)

A. \(1:1\).
B. \(1:3\).
C. \(2:3\).
D. \(4:9\).

If the area of a regular \(10\)-sided polygon is \(123\text{ cm}^2\), find the length of the side of the \(10\)-sided polygon. Give the answer correct to the nearest \(0.1\text{ cm}\).
A. \(3.9\text{ cm}\)
B. \(4.0\text{ cm}\)
C. \(6.8\text{ cm}\)
D. \(8.0\text{ cm}\)

For \(0^\circ\le x\le90^\circ\), the least value of \(4-2\cos x\) is
A. \(0\).
B. \(1\).
C. \(2\).
D. \(4\).

In the figure, find \(AC\) correct to \(2\) decimal places.

A. \(5.04\)
B. \(9.17\)
C. \(11.14\)
D. \(15.62\)

In the figure, \(\sin x=\)

A. \(\dfrac{4}{3}\).
B. \(\dfrac{3}{4}\).
C. \(\dfrac{3}{5}\).
D. \(\dfrac{4}{5}\).

In the figure, \(O\) is the centre of the circle \(ABCD\). If \(E\), \(A\), \(B\) and \(E\), \(D\), \(C\) are straight lines and \(EA=AO\), find \(\angle AEO\).

A. \(18^\circ\)
B. \(24^\circ\)
C. \(27^\circ\)
D. \(36^\circ\)

In the figure, \(O\) is the centre of the circle \(ABC\). Find \(x\).

A. \(17.5^\circ\)
B. \(27.5^\circ\)
C. \(35^\circ\)
D. \(55^\circ\)

In the figure, \(ABCD\) is a circle. \(AC\) and \(BD\) meet at \(E\). If \(AD=4\), \(AE=2\), \(EC=5\) and \(BE=4\), then \(BC=\)

A. \(6\).
B. \(7\).
C. \(8\).
D. \(10\).

In the figure, \(ABC\) is a circle. If \(\angle ABC=30^\circ\) and \(AC=4\), then the circumference of the circle is

A. \(24\).
B. \(48\).
C. \(8\pi\).
D. \(16\pi\).

In the figure, \(ABC\) is a straight line. If \(BD\mathbin{\!/\mkern-5mu/\!}CE\), then \(\angle DCE=\)

A. \(56^\circ\).
B. \(68^\circ\).
C. \(112^\circ\).
D. \(124^\circ\).

In the figure, \(ABCD\) and \(AGFE\) are straight lines. If \(BC=2\text{ cm}\), \(CD=3\text{ cm}\), \(BG=6\text{ cm}\) and \(CF=10\text{ cm}\), then \(DE=\)

A. \(12\text{ cm}\).
B. \(14\text{ cm}\).
C. \(15\text{ cm}\).
D. \(16\text{ cm}\).

If \(a\gt0\), \(b\gt0\) and \(c\lt0\), which of the following may represent the graph of the straight line \(ax+by+c=0\)?

A.
B.
C.
D.

In the figure, \(L_1\) and \(L_2\) are two straight lines intersecting at a point on the \(y\)-axis. If the equation of \(L_1\) is \(x+2y-2=0\), then the equation of \(L_2\) is

A. \(2x-y+1=0\).
B. \(2x-y-2=0\).
C. \(2x+y+1=0\).
D. \(2x+y-2=0\).

The mean weight of \(36\) boys and \(32\) girls is \(46\text{ kg}\). If the mean weight of the boys is \(52\text{ kg}\), then the mean weight of the girls is
A. \(39.25\text{ kg}\).
B. \(40\text{ kg}\).
C. \(40.67\text{ kg}\).
D. \(49\text{ kg}\).

A bag contains \(3\) red balls and \(4\) green balls. If two balls are drawn randomly from the bag one by one without replacement, then the probability that the two balls are of different colours is
A. \(\dfrac{2}{7}\).
B. \(\dfrac{4}{7}\).
C. \(\dfrac{12}{49}\).
D. \(\dfrac{24}{49}\).

Peter and May each throws a dart. The probability of Peter's hitting the target is \(0.2\). The probability of May's hitting the target is \(0.3\). Find the probability of at least one dart hitting the target.
A. \(0.38\)
B. \(0.44\)
C. \(0.50\)
D. \(0.56\)

The pie chart below shows the expenditure of a student in March 2004. If the student spent \(\$520\) on meals, then the student's total expenditure on entertainment and clothing was

A. \(\$780\).
B. \(\$1092\).
C. \(\$1352\).
D. \(\$1872\).

David got \(70\) marks in a test and his standard score was \(-0.625\). If the standard deviation of the test marks was \(8\) marks, then the mean mark of the test was
A. \(62\) marks.
B. \(65\) marks.
C. \(75\) marks.
D. \(78\) marks.

It is known that the equation \(2^x=12x-9\) has only one root in the interval \(-3\le x\le -2\). The method of bisection is used to find the root starting with the interval \(-3\le x\le -2\). After the first approximation, the interval which contains the root becomes \(-3\le x\le -2.5\). Find the interval which contains the root after the third approximation.
A. \(-2.625\le x\le -2.5\)
B. \(-2.75\le x\le -2.625\)
C. \(-2.875\le x\le -2.75\)
D. \(-3\le x\le -2.875\)

Which of the following shaded regions may represent the solution of \(x\le y-2\)?

A.
B.
C.
D.

If \(81,a,b,3\) is a geometric sequence, then \(b-a=\)
A. \(-18\).
B. \(18\).
C. \(-26\).
D. \(26\).

In the figure, \(OAB\) is a sector. The perimeter and the area of the sector are \(x\text{ cm}\) and \(y\text{ cm}^2\) respectively. If \(x=y\), then \(AB=\)

A. \(5\text{ cm}\).
B. \(10\text{ cm}\).
C. \(\dfrac{5\pi}{3}\text{ cm}\).
D. \(\dfrac{10\pi}{3}\text{ cm}\).

\(\dfrac{\cos\theta-1}{\cos\theta\sin\theta}=\)
A. \(-\tan\theta\).
B. \(\tan\theta\).
C. \(-\dfrac{\sin\theta}{\cos\theta}\).
D. \(\dfrac{\cos\theta-1}{\sin\theta\cos\theta}\).

If \(A+B=\pi\), which of the following must be true?
I. \(\sin A=\sin B\)
II. \(\cos A=\sin B\)
III. \(\cos A=\cos B\)
A. I only.
B. II only.
C. I and III only.
D. II and III only.

The figure shows the cube \(ABCDEFGH\) of side \(2\text{ cm}\). \(X\) and \(Y\) are the mid-points of \(AB\) and \(GH\) respectively. Find \(XY\).

A. \(3\text{ cm}\)
B. \(2\sqrt{2}\text{ cm}\)
C. \(\sqrt{7}\text{ cm}\)
D. \(\sqrt{5}\text{ cm}\)

In the figure, \(ABCDEFGH\) is a rectangular block. \(EG\) and \(FH\) meet at \(X\). \(M\) is the mid-point of \(EH\). Which of the following makes the greatest angle with the plane \(ABCD\)?

A. \(AG\)
B. \(AH\)
C. \(AM\)
D. \(AX\)

In the figure, \(ABCD\) is a circle. If \(CD=2\), \(DA=2\), \(AB=2\) and \(BC=2\), then \(x=\)

A. \(108^\circ\).
B. \(112^\circ\).
C. \(120^\circ\).
D. \(144^\circ\).

In the figure, \(TS\), \(SR\) and \(QP\) are tangents to the circle at \(T\), \(R\) and \(P\) respectively. If \(TS\mathbin{\!/\mkern-5mu/\!}PQ\), \(TS=3\) and \(QP=12\), then the radius of the circle is

A. \(4.5\).
B. \(6\).
C. \(7.5\).
D. \(9\).

If the straight line \(x+y-3=0\) divides the circle \(x^2+y^2+2x-ky-4=0\) into two equal parts, then \(k=\)
A. \(-4\).
B. \(4\).
C. \(-8\).
D. \(8\).

The equation of a circle is \(x^2+y^2-4x+2y+1=0\). Which of the following is/are true?
I. The circle touches the \(y\)-axis.
II. The origin lies outside the circle.
III. The centre of the circle lies in the second quadrant.
A. II only
B. III only
C. I and II only
D. I and III only

The mean deviation of the four numbers \(x-8\), \(x-2\), \(x+3\) and \(x+7\) is
A. \(x\).
B. \(0\).
C. \(5\).
D. \(5.6\).
